title: Remove references to deleted AI maker modals + generation routes (character/persona/lorebook/preset)
description:
WORST failure mode: the skill tells users to use UI/routes deleted in 2.0.
Skill says: character-cards.md (API Endpoints) 'POST /api/character-maker/generate'; lorebooks.md (AI Lorebook Maker) + 'POST /api/lorebook-maker/generate'; architecture.md (API Surface, ~line 149) lists /api/character-maker, /api/lorebook-maker, /api/persona-maker; decision-guide.md implies these flows.
Reality (2.0): the standalone character/persona/lorebook maker modals AND their dedicated generation routes were REMOVED (CHANGELOG [2.0.0] Removed); the Preset editor's standalone review tab + preset-review route were also removed. No *-maker routes exist; packages/server/src/routes/index.ts:55,107 registers only professorMariWorkspaceRoutes at /api/professor-mari/workspace. AI-assisted creation moved to Professor Mari (seed-mari.ts command set).
Nuance: plain MANUAL create forms (e.g. CreateCharacterModal.tsx) still exist - do NOT remove manual-creation references; only the AI maker modals/routes are gone.
Fix: delete every maker-route/endpoint reference across those files; replace with 'AI-assisted creation is done via Professor Mari on the Home screen.'

title: Rewrite Professor Mari: home-page workspace assistant, not an active conversation character
description:
Skill says: character-cards.md (Professor Mari Pattern) injects MARI_ASSISTANT_PROMPT 'when she's the active character in a conversation'; SKILL.md and architecture.md omit Mari's 2.0 role.
Reality (2.0): Mari is a separate Home-screen assistant backed by a workspace agent; her seed card says she 'no longer appears as a normal Conversation-mode chat character' (packages/server/src/db/seed-mari.ts:18; client HomeProfessorMariChat.tsx; packages/server/src/services/professor-mari/workspace-agent.service.ts). MARI_ASSISTANT_PROMPT injection is still gated by PROFESSOR_MARI_ID (generate.routes.ts:2962-2964); the isBuiltInAssistant flag independently drives some scenario handling (character-prompt-context.ts:62; conversation.routes.ts:491), so the 'setting the flag does nothing' claim is partly wrong.
Fix: rewrite the Mari pattern in character-cards.md and add a Mari section to SKILL.md + architecture.md.

title: Contributor workflow targets `staging`, not `main`
description:
Skill says: SKILL.md line 237 'Branch from main'; sync ritual lines 293-294 use 'git checkout main' / 'git pull origin main'; repo-fetch (lines 24,450) hardcodes /main/.
Reality: CONTRIBUTING.md:34 'Active development happens on staging, not main'; :56 'Base your feature branch on staging'; :57 'Open PRs against staging'. main is release-only.
Impact: following the skill makes contributors branch from / PR against the wrong base.
Fix: branch from/target staging; update the sync ritual; note current behavior lives on staging (fetch staging to verify), main only for released behavior.

title: Validation reference lists `pnpm db:push`, which no longer exists
description:
Skill says: SKILL.md line 400 'pnpm db:push  # sync SQLite schema'.
Reality: no db:push script in root package.json; docs/FILE_STORAGE_MIGRATION.md:29 says users should NOT need pnpm db:push or db:migrate - storage is file-native.
Fix: remove pnpm db:push from the validation reference.

title: Storage is file-native JSON (DATA_DIR/storage), not SQLite
description:
Skill says: architecture.md Data Storage (lines 119-132) 'SQLite via Drizzle ORM... Single SQLite file (default data/marinara-engine.db)... Backing up = copying the .db file'; custom-tool-webhook.template.md section 6 'stored in the user's local SQLite DB'; SKILL.md sync ritual line 300 restores from packages/server/data.
Reality: STORAGE_BACKEND defaults to 'files' (docs/CONFIGURATION.md:23 - sqlite is legacy opt-in); packages/server/src/db/file-backed-store.ts is the live store (SQLite opened only for one-time legacy import); README.md:166 'Fully local file-native storage.' Data lives under DATA_DIR/storage.
Fix: describe file-native JSON storage as default; fix backup wording and the test-install data-restore path.

title: Version-drift table omits 2 of the 10 CI-checked files
description:
Skill says: SKILL.md version-drift table (lines 416-426) lists 8 version-bearing files.
Reality: scripts/versioning.mjs:124-161 covers 10 files; the skill omits packages/client/public/manifest.json (PWA manifest version) and README.md ('Current stable release' line). pnpm version:check (CI) fails if either drifts.
Fix: add packages/client/public/manifest.json and README.md to the table.

title: Script custom tools are disabled by default (CUSTOM_TOOL_SCRIPT_ENABLED)
description:
Skill says: custom-tools.md (script) and decision-guide.md section 4 recommend the 'script' execution type with no mention of an enable gate.
Reality: script execution is OFF unless CUSTOM_TOOL_SCRIPT_ENABLED=true; executeCustomTool returns { error: 'Script custom tools are disabled. Set CUSTOM_TOOL_SCRIPT_ENABLED=true...' } when off (tool-executor.ts:335-340; gate runtime-config.ts:485-487).
Fix: state that script tools require CUSTOM_TOOL_SCRIPT_ENABLED=true (default off) in both files.

title: Webhook tools are HTTPS-only + SSRF-blocked; localhost needs WEBHOOK_LOCAL_URLS_ENABLED
description:
Skill says: custom-tools.md (webhook); custom-tool-webhook.template.md section 5 tells users to test against 'localhost:3100' - REJECTED by default.
Reality: webhooks use safeFetch with allowedProtocols ['https:'] and block loopback/private/reserved IPs unless WEBHOOK_LOCAL_URLS_ENABLED=true (tool-executor.ts:307-322; SSRF enforcement security.ts:309-338; flag runtime-config.ts:481-483); response capped 512KB.
Fix: document HTTPS-only + WEBHOOK_LOCAL_URLS_ENABLED + 512KB cap; rewrite the template testing instructions.

title: Custom tool timeout is 60s configurable, not 10s (webhook) / 5s (script)
description:
Skill says: custom-tools.md webhook '10-second timeout', script '5-second timeout'; template sections 4/6; decision-guide.md section 4 '5-second timeout'.
Reality: both share one configurable timeout getCustomToolTimeoutMs(), default DEFAULT_CUSTOM_TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS=60_000ms, env CUSTOM_TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS (runtime-config.ts:23,465-467; tool-executor.ts:298,316,360-363).
Fix: replace the 10s/5s split with a single configurable 60s default.

title: Built-in agent count is 21, not ~25; remove 6 retired/nonexistent agents
description:
Skill says: agents.md lists ~25 agents incl prompt-reviewer, schedule-planner, response-orchestrator, autonomous-messenger, consistency-editor, chat-summary; architecture.md line 57 '25 built-ins'.
Reality: BUILT_IN_AGENT_MANIFESTS = 21 (packages/shared/src/features/agents/agent-registry.generated.ts:27-49; BUILT_IN_AGENT_IDS types/agent.ts:383-405). RETIRED_BUILT_IN_AGENT_IDS retires prompt-reviewer, response-orchestrator, schedule-planner, chat-summary, autonomous-messenger, youtube, secret-plot-driver (types/agent.ts:407-415). consistency-editor never existed (rewrite = continuity + prose-guardian); chat-summary is only the DEFAULT_CHAT_SUMMARY_PROMPT constant.
Fix: rebuild the list from the registry; count = 21 (agents.md + architecture.md).

title: Fix agent ids/phases and add missing built-ins (agents.md)
description:
Skill says: agents.md uses display-style slugs and wrong phases.
Reality (manifests + types/agent.ts:383-405): continuity ('Continuity Checker') is post_processing and phase-LOCKED (types/agent.ts:30) - skill puts it in pre-generation; illustrator is post_processing (runInterval 5), not parallel; spotify ('Music DJ') is post_processing, Spotify+YouTube (skill calls it 'spotify-dj' parallel); toy-control is id 'haptic' ('Haptic Feedback', Intiface Central), not 'love-toys-control'/Buttplug.io; immersive-html is id 'html' ('Immersive HTML'), pre_generation, runtimeDisabled. MISSING: knowledge-router (pre_generation), card-evolution-auditor (post_processing). Use exact ids from BUILT_IN_AGENT_IDS.
Fix: correct ids/phases, note continuity+prose-guardian force-pinned post_processing, add the 2 missing agents.

title: Custom agent field is `resultType` (not `agentResultType`); enum outdated
description:
Skill says: agents.md + assets/custom-agent.template.json use 'agentResultType' with an old enum (chat_summary, prompt_review, etc.).
Reality: the field is 'resultType' (agent.schema.ts:51). The v2.0 enum (agent.schema.ts:9-36; types/agent.ts:35-61) has 26 values: chat_summary and prompt_review are GONE; new values include character_card_update, custom_tracker_update, youtube_control, haptic_command, cyoa_choices, game_master_narration, party_action, game_map_update, game_state_transition, prompt_patch, frontend_theme_update. Schema also has imagePath; template missing resultType + imagePath.
Fix: rename field to resultType, regenerate enum, add imagePath+resultType to the template.

title: agents.md API endpoints are wrong (toggle path/method, nonexistent PUT replace)
description:
Skill says: agents.md lists 'PUT /api/agents/:id' (replace) and 'POST /api/agents/:id/toggle'.
Reality: no PUT /api/agents/:id. Toggle is PUT /api/agents/toggle/:agentType (agents.routes.ts:283). PATCH at /:id and /type/:agentType (234,225). Missing: /images/file/:filename, /runs/:chatId/custom, /cadence/:agentType/:chatId, /runs/:runId, /:id/image, /echo-messages/:chatId, /runs/:chatId, /memory/:agentType/:chatId (agents.routes.ts:136-406).
Fix: correct toggle, drop PUT-replace, add missing endpoints.

title: character-cards.md API endpoints are wrong (import/export paths)
description:
Skill says: character-cards.md lists 'POST /api/characters/import' and 'GET /api/characters/:id/export as JSON or PNG'.
Reality: no /api/characters/import; import is /api/import/* (import.routes.ts:697,782,602 - e.g. /api/import/st-character, /api/import/marinara). Export is two endpoints: GET /:id/export (format qs) and GET /:id/export-png (characters.routes.ts:620,738). Groups at /groups/list and /groups/:id. Missing: /:id/versions, /:id/duplicate, /:id/gallery, /:id/avatar, /export-bulk.
Fix: correct import/export paths; mark the list non-exhaustive.

title: Lorebook scope model is wrong (isGlobal/personaId/scope object)
description:
Skill says: lorebooks.md describes scope as global / characterId / chatId, with global = 'both ids null'.
Reality: lorebook.schema.ts:83-91 has isGlobal, characterId, characterIds[], personaId, personaIds[], chatId, plus a scope object { mode: all|disabled|specific, chatIds: [] }. Global is isGlobal:true; persona-scoped + multi-character/persona lorebooks exist; superRefine forbids isGlobal + targets (125-154).
Fix: rewrite the scope section; add persona-scoped lorebooks (new in 2.0).

title: Lorebook entry field values wrong (position, selectiveLogic)
description:
Skill says: lorebooks.md 'position | 0 or 1'; 'selectiveLogic | and|or|not'; selective example 'both primary AND secondary' using 'and'.
Reality: position is 0..2 (2 = inject at message depth) (lorebook.schema.ts:179; types/lorebook.ts:159; prompt-injector.ts:28-44); selectiveLogic enum is [and, and_all, or, not, not_all] (lorebook.schema.ts:16); strict all-secondary-keys is and_all, not and (ST import maps 3 -> and_all, st-lorebook.importer.ts:210-218).
Fix: document position 0/1/2, the 5 selectiveLogic values, and correct the selective example to and_all.

title: architecture.md mischaracterizes the local model runtime
description:
Skill says: architecture.md line 158 'Google Gemma 4 E2B built in via MLX sidecar... mainly for tracker agents & scene analysis'.
Reality: 2.0's Local Model runtime starts llama.cpp with --jinja for OpenAI-compatible NATIVE tool calls (CHANGELOG [2.0.0] Added; sidecar-launch-plan.ts:36-37). Backend defaults llama_cpp (mlx only on Apple Silicon) and runs arbitrary user GGUF models (sidecar-model.service.ts:261) - not a single built-in Gemma, and it does native tool calling, not just trackers/scene analysis.
Fix: rewrite the local-model bullet.

title: Extensions execution model + storage changed (server-persisted, ES-module loader, apiFetch denylist)
description:
Skill says: extensions.md - executed via new Function('marinara', ext.js); stored in useUIStore.installedExtensions; apiFetch hits any /api/*.
Reality: 2.0 builds an ES module (buildExtensionModuleSource), serves it via Blob/URL.createObjectURL, dynamically import()s it; the marinara API comes from globalThis.__marinaraExtensionApis by key (CustomThemeInjector.tsx:28-43,248-252). Extensions persist server-side via /api/extensions CRUD (extensions.routes.ts; use-extensions.ts:46-86), with useUIStore.installedExtensions now a legacy localStorage list one-shot migrated then cleared (88-148). apiFetch rejects /extensions(/*) and /admin(/*) (CustomThemeInjector.tsx:183-202).
Fix: update execution-model, storage/distribution, and apiFetch-denylist sections.

title: SKILL.md: refresh stale version/maturity phrasing
description:
Skill says: SKILL.md uses 'does feature X exist in v1.5 yet?' (line 135) and 'v1.5.5' (line 450) as examples, and calls the engine 'under active alpha development' (line 169).
Reality: current release is 2.0.0 (package.json:3); the project ships a stable 2.0 line.
Fix: bump the example version strings to the 2.0 line; reword 'alpha' to 'actively developed, on its 2.0 stable line'. Harmless but dates the skill.

title: architecture.md: refresh provider/route lists and stale counts
description:
Skill says: architecture.md Providers (line 156) 'OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral, Cohere, OpenRouter, NanoGPT'; Bot Browser (line 150) omits DataCat; 'generate.routes.ts (6,800+ lines)'.
Reality: providers.ts also defines xai ('xAI / Grok', 114-117), claude_subscription (48-59), openai_chatgpt (28-38), google_vertex (69-77). bot-browser-datacat.routes.ts registered at index.ts:95. generate.routes.ts ~10,846 lines.
Fix: add missing providers + DataCat; update or drop the line-count note (built-in count handled separately).

title: lorebooks.md: verify token-budget tie-break; optionally name embedding model
description:
Skill says: lorebooks.md (Token Budget) 'ordered by order first, then entry length / internal logic.' The 'then entry length' tie-break is unverified.
Reality: order is 'lower = earlier' (types/lorebook.ts:163-164); budget-skip visibility is a 1.6/2.0 feature; embedding model is Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (local-embedder.ts:20).
Fix: re-derive the eviction order from prompt-injector/activation, mention budget-skip visibility, optionally name the embedding model. Low impact.

title: Document Game Mode as shipped + Conversation UNO/turn-games
description:
Skill says: architecture.md line 95 'Game Mode (coming soon, partially live in v1.5.x)'; Conversation Mode list omits turn-games.
Reality: Game Mode is a fully shipped 2.0 mode (HUD/tracker field locks, Game-mode custom-agent selection, setup wizard, checkpoint restore, journal serialization - CHANGELOG [2.0.0]); routes /api/game, /api/game-assets (index.ts:101-102). 2.0 added UNO/turn-game support for Conversation chats (turn-games.routes.ts, /api/turn-games, index.ts:103).
Fix: remove 'coming soon', document Game Mode features, add Conversation turn-games.

title: Document Universal Preset v12 default + Conversation/Game preset prompts
description:
Skill says: architecture.md (Preset, lines 50-51) frames presets as roleplay-only and states no version.
Reality: 2.0 set Universal Preset v12 as default (default-preset.json:9 'v12'); presets now include Conversation + Game mode prompts (CHANGELOG [2.0.0] Highlights).
Fix: note v12 default and the Conversation/Game preset prompts.

title: Update prompt-assembly description for the 2.0 rework
description:
Skill says: architecture.md (Prompt Assembly, lines 98-117) describes the old pipeline (post-history instructions glued to the system prompt, etc.).
Reality (CHANGELOG [2.0.0] Changed/Fixed): post-history system sections now injected as user-side content at a configured depth (metadata preserved) instead of glued to the pre-history system prompt; max-context enforcement prioritizes non-history material then windows history; added assistant prefill steering + visible tracker context; lorebook placement reworked.
Fix: update the pipeline steps accordingly.

title: Document native tool calling for local models (llama.cpp --jinja toggle)
description:
Skill says: custom-tools.md implies tools are always sent as OpenAI-format function definitions; decision-guide.md omits local-model tool-call support.
Reality: for the Local Model (llama.cpp sidecar) runtime, native OpenAI-compatible tool calls only work with --jinja, gated by enableNativeToolCalls (sidecar-launch-plan.ts:35-37; sidecar-model.service.ts:172-175,546). Without it, custom tools won't fire on local models.
Fix: note in custom-tools.md + decision-guide.md that local-model tool calling requires enabling the native-tool-calls runtime toggle.

title: Document folder-based import/export for custom agents & extensions
description:
Skill says: agents.md presents custom-agent creation only via the editor; extensions.md says distribution is manual JSON/paste.
Reality: 2.0 added folder-based import/export for custom agents AND browser extensions (CHANGELOG [2.0.0] lines 8,17,23). Extensions export as a zip with a marinara-extensions.json envelope (kind 'marinara.extension-folder') + per-extension manifest.json/extension.css/extension.js (client lib/extension-transfer.ts:13-65).
Fix: add export/import (single-JSON + folder/zip) subsections to agents.md and extensions.md.

title: Document custom-agent capability model, Turn Data Access, Game Mode agent selection
description:
Skill says: agents.md describes a custom agent as just 'a scoped LLM call'; omits capabilities, turn-data opt-in, mode gating.
Reality: 2.0 adds CUSTOM_AGENT_CAPABILITY_IDS (create_lorebooks, edit_lorebooks, edit_messages, edit_trackers, change_frontend_styling, trigger_image_generation, access_vectors, edit_main_prompt) derived from settings.customCapabilities/resultType/enabledTools (types/agent.ts:467-535); opt-in Turn Data Access gives post-processing agents preGenInjections+parallelResults (types/agent.ts:369-372; issue #778); Game Mode custom-agent selection + per-agent modeAllowlist gating (CHANGELOG [2.0.0]).
Fix: add capability-model, Turn Data Access, and Game-mode/modeAllowlist subsections.

title: Document Mari's expanded creation scope (agents/extensions/themes) - verify against source
description:
CHANGELOG [2.0.0] line 12 says Mari can create agents + extensions and change the theme. workspace-agent.service.ts:238-247 shows 'mari extensions/themes/agents/db' ops on installed_extensions, agent_configs, custom_tools. BUT the seed-mari.ts prompt command set observed in audit only shows [create_character]/[create_persona]/<create_lorebook>/<create_preset>/[create_chat] + [navigate].
Action: resolve the discrepancy against source (does Mari directly create agents/extensions/themes, or navigate to those panels?), then document Mari's true creation scope in extensions.md + agents.md. Builds on the Mari rewrite issue.

title: Document native appearance customization + synced custom themes (reduces need for custom CSS)
description:
Skill says: extensions.md and decision-guide.md section 6 recommend a custom-CSS extension for theming/accent/background tweaks.
Reality: 2.0 rebuilt appearance customization natively in Settings -> Appearance: app background color + gradient presets, accent color, accent RGB mode, accent pulse, chat text/chrome text colors, font family/size, and resetAppearanceSettings() (client SettingsPanel.tsx AppearanceSettings; CHANGELOG [2.0.0] lines 13,26). Plus a server-synced custom-themes system (/api/themes, use-themes.ts, CustomThemeInjector.tsx:56-79).
Fix: note these are native Settings; reserve custom-CSS extensions for structural/DOM changes Settings can't express.

title: Update built-in tools list + document includeHiddenContext (custom-tools.md)
description:
Skill says: custom-tools.md Built-In Tools lists roll_dice, update_game_state, set_expression, trigger_event, search_lorebook + 5 Spotify tools; schema omits includeHiddenContext; script sandbox 'exposes ONLY' omits context.
Reality: the executor switch registers ~17 built-ins (tool-executor.ts:220-282) incl save_lorebook_entry, edit_chat_message, read_chat_summary, append_chat_summary, read_chat_variable, write_chat_variable, spotify_get_current_playback (6 Spotify tools). createCustomToolSchema includes includeHiddenContext (custom-tool.schema.ts:20); when true the webhook POST body gains a context field and the script sandbox gains a context global (tool-executor.ts:308-315,347).
Fix: rebuild the built-in tools list, add includeHiddenContext to the schema, document the context body/global, fix template storage/HTTPS notes.

title: Expand lorebooks.md + template: vectorization, trigger types, recursion, filters, folders, missing fields
description:
lorebooks.md + assets/lorebook-entry.template.json miss 2.0 capabilities (all from packages/shared/src/schemas/lorebook.schema.ts unless noted):
- excludeFromVectorization ('No Vector') at entry (200) and lorebook (82) level; per-entry embedding (types/lorebook.ts:209-212).
- Trigger types: generationTriggerFilterMode/Filters chat|continue|autonomous (176-177; client LorebookEntryRow.tsx:163-170); guided + guided-manual replies now use 'Chat reply' triggers (CHANGELOG).
- Recursion: excludeRecursion, delayUntilRecursion (191-193) in addition to preventRecursion.
- Filters: characterFilterMode/Ids, characterTagFilterMode/Filters, additionalMatchingSources (172-178; enum 20-28).
- Folders: folderId (190) + create/updateLorebookFolderSchema (53-65).
- Entry description (160); lorebook-level entryLimit, imagePath, tags[], generatedBy, sourceAgentId (71-93).
Fix: document these + update the template; note keyword testing + improved ST lorebook mapping.

title: Update Knowledge Retrieval section for 2.0 (knowledge-router, sources, embedding connection)
description:
Skill says: lorebooks.md (Knowledge Retrieval) - create a knowledge_retrieval agent; 'entries embedded automatically when the agent is configured.'
Reality: knowledge-retrieval lives at packages/server/src/services/agents/knowledge-retrieval.ts (path ok), but 2.0 adds a separate knowledge-router built-in (lower-cost RAG), knowledge-sources routes, and knowledge-agent-settings; embedding requires a configured embedding connection (Local Model sidecar /api/sidecar/v1/embeddings) and is governed by excludeFromVectorization - not 'automatic when the agent is configured.'
Fix: verify the exact agent type string and rewrite the section to cover knowledge sources/router + the embedding-connection requirement.

title: Document character extension fields (rpgStats, typed display fields) + granular ST import
description:
Skill says: character-cards.md documents extensions talkativeness/fav/world/depth_prompt/backstory/appearance and treats nameColor/dialogueColor/boxColor/conversationStatus as 'passthrough extras'; the template omits the display fields; ST import described as one monolithic migration.
Reality: the typed CharacterExtensions interface (types/character.ts:42-62) includes rpgStats (RPGStatsConfig: enabled, custom attributes, hp) and declares nameColor/dialogueColor/boxColor/conversationStatus as first-class fields. ST import is now granular per-type endpoints + bulk scan/run (import.routes.ts:467-908). DataCat is in the Bot Browser (bot-browser-datacat.routes.ts).
Fix: add rpgStats + typed display fields to docs + template; note granular ST import + DataCat.
